criteria.name: Hook strength
criteria.note: Concrete five-day deadline plus named live tension between executive and legislative branches; hints at the asymmetry that drives the post.

criteria.name: Local relevance
criteria.note: Every claim is anchored to named AK actors, places, and bills (DNR, Dalton Highway, Stak, SB 250, HB 217, HB 239, Tobin, Carrick).

criteria.name: Factual density
criteria.note: Specific dates, vote counts, dollar figures, gigawatts, and deadlines appear in nearly every sentence; one paragraph stacks three bills tightly.

criteria.name: Source quality
criteria.note: All claims trace to verified findings and Northern Journal is named, but inline attribution for the bill actions to akleg.gov is implicit rather than explicit.

criteria.name: Voice match
criteria.note: Analytical, structural, position-taking — 'speed gap,' 'asymmetry itself,' '50-year lease runs longer than the political cycle' all read like the anchor.

criteria.name: Readability (mobile)
criteria.note: Paragraphs are mostly tight but the three-bill paragraph is a dense block that's harder to scan on phone; total length is at the high end of target.

criteria.name: Engagement question
criteria.note: Real, debatable binary tied directly to the post's structural tension between executive lease-making and legislative pace; not generic.
